region_of_origin: Global — yellowfin tuna (Thunnus albacares) is widely distributed in tropical and subtropical oceans; sold as sashimi-grade in Singapore at Don Don Donki, Meidi-Ya, and wet market fishmongers
food_subgroup: Lean fish, raw — very high protein (~24g per 100g), very low fat (~1g per 100g), zero carbohydrate

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for raw yellowfin tuna as a food product; SNOMED CT coverage for specific raw fish species is limited.

serving.description: 100g raw tuna steak (reference standard) or approximately 5 sashimi slices (~80g)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard reference serving is 100g of raw yellowfin tuna steak; a typical sashimi portion of 5 slices weighs approximately 80g.

nutrient_source.title: Yellowfin Tuna, Fresh, Raw — USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 175159)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Direct match to USDA SR Legacy entry for yellowfin tuna, fresh, raw. All per-100g nutrient values sourced from this entry. Confidence: very high.

gi_source.title: N/A — food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Yellowfin tuna raw contains 0g carbohydrate per 100g (USDA SR Legacy FDC 175159). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. No glycaemic response is expected. GL per serving = 0.0 by definition. This aligns with the University of Sydney GI Database's general approach for pure protein/fat foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (39mg per 100g) and high potassium (444mg per 100g) supporting a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio
hypertension.guidance: Yellowfin tuna raw is an excellent protein choice for hypertensive patients: sodium is very low at 39mg per 100g, potassium is high at 444mg per 100g, and the virtually fat-free lean protein profile supports cardiovascular health without adding sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate, GI not applicable, very high protein-to-calorie ratio (24.4g protein per 109 kcal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Yellowfin tuna raw is highly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management: it contains zero carbohydrate and therefore produces no glycaemic response, while its exceptional protein density (24.4g per 100g) supports satiety and lean body mass preservation without raising blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.3g per 100g), moderate omega-3 (EPA 0.01g + DHA 0.25g = ~0.26g per 100g), high selenium (90.6mcg per 100g = ~165% RDA)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Yellowfin tuna raw is a heart-healthy lean protein with very low saturated fat (0.3g per 100g) and low total fat (1g per 100g); note that omega-3 content (~0.26g EPA+DHA per 100g) is significantly lower than salmon (~1.8g), so it should not be relied upon as a primary omega-3 source, but its high selenium and niacin content support antioxidant and lipid metabolism pathways relevant to cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High selenium (90.6mcg per 100g) as antioxidant cofactor in glutathione peroxidase; moderate omega-3
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Yellowfin tuna raw provides very high selenium (90.6mcg per 100g, approximately 165% of the daily RDA), which supports antioxidant pathways via glutathione peroxidase and may assist in managing inflammatory burden in rheumatoid arthritis, though omega-3 content is moderate rather than high.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yellowfin Tuna Steak (Raw) — per 100g raw: 109 kcal, 24.4g protein, 1.0g fat, 0g carbohydrate, 39mg sodium, 444mg potassium. Yellowfin tuna (Thunnus albacares) is a large pelagic fish with deep red to pink flesh, firm and silky texture, and a clean, mild oceanic flavour prized in Japanese sashimi cuisine. It is sold sashimi-grade at Don Don Donki, Meidi-Ya, and wet market fishmongers in Singapore. CRITICAL NUTRITION NOTE: yellowfin tuna is NOT a high-omega-3 fish — EPA 0.01g + DHA 0.25g = approximately 0.26g EPA+DHA per 100g, which is approximately 7 times lower than Atlantic salmon (~1.8g). It should not be recommended as a primary omega-3 source. Mercury content is approximately 0.35ppm — pregnant women and young children should limit consumption to 2 servings per week per health authority guidelines. Exceptional selenium content: 90.6mcg per 100g = approximately 165% of the daily RDA, supporting antioxidant and thyroid function. Very high niacin (18.8mg = ~118% RDA). Cooking yield: 100g raw yields approximately 70g cooked. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175159. Confidence: very high. Suitable for diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management due to zero carbohydrate, very low sodium, and very low saturated fat profile.

confidence_reason: All nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 175159 (Yellowfin Tuna, Fresh, Raw), an approved primary source with exact match to the food described. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated from this single authoritative entry.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — yellowfin tuna is a pure protein-and-fat food with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 175159. Sugar_g is naturally zero by the same basis. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as fish contains no plant fibre. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed trace/negligible per USDA SR Legacy). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0g carbohydrate per 100g); gi_category set to not_applicable and glycemic_index set to null per Veritas protocol for zero-carbohydrate foods. Monounsaturated fat (0.17g) and polyunsaturated fat (0.27g) values sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 175159 fatty acid breakdown. Water content (74.03g) sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC 175159. Mercury advisory: approximately 0.35ppm — pregnant women and young children should limit to 2 servings per week. Omega-3 note: EPA+DHA approximately 0.26g per 100g — significantly lower than salmon; tuna should not be promoted as a high-omega-3 food. SNOMED CT code not available for raw yellowfin tuna as a food product; snomed_ct_match_type set to none with gap note. Tamil local name added: மஞ்சள் துடுப்பு சூரை மீன் (பச்சை) — yellowfin tuna (raw) in Tamil. Hindi local name added: पीले पंख वाली टूना मछली (कच्ची) — yellowfin fin tuna fish (raw) in Hindi. These are provided to satisfy G6 multilingual compliance for Singapore-context records where Tamil and Hindi are mandatory language fields. All aliases_by_language entries verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
